tax: Placed at genus rank, Terrae is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a bipedal fault-pressure stone body distinguishable from the Boulder-Kin by its persistent humanoid form and orbital glow. A new species belongs here only if it shows a bipedal stone body with its own fault-pressure formation mechanism, stone-type composition, and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a roughly humanoid stone mass with orbital glows, ranging 1.2 m (minor) to 6 m (major). A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — orbital amber glow, plinth-impressions rather than round prints, and a double-beat floor-shake different from a Galeb Dur's single roll — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Terrae occupies fault zones and fault convergences throughout the Throat's lower tiers. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where fault pressure — shallow, deep, or convergent — animates a stone mass into a bipedal form and recur with the fault re-pressurises and the same stone mass re-forms, sustaining themselves on fault pressure at various depths and types.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: all Terrae forms are territorial, set themselves rigid when at rest, and sense approach through the floor; they differ in size, speed, stone type, and the complexity of their response. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a bipedal stone body, amber orbital glow, plinth-foot impressions, fault pressure dependence, and a vibration-failure mode specific to each form. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: mass strikes, stone incorporation, floor-tremor sensing, and rigid-stone disguise; the nobilis form adds digit precision, vibration redirection, and fold-communication. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Terrae matters because the Terrae genus accounts for most grey-folk fatalities attributed to 'the rock coming alive' and is the primary subject of Stonesong Herald classification work. It lets survey reports sort humanoid stone hazards by size and stone type, enabling a crew to identify which form is active and plan accordingly without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Terrae is the genus-rank grouping of roughly humanoid stone-body elementals of the fault-pressure kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a bipedal stone body held by fault pressure, with a featureless or faceted head-mass and orbital glows distinguishing them from boulders and columns, and divide below into species groups by the depth and type of fault pressure — shallow/compact (minor), standard (single fault), massive/dark (major), or metamorphic/deep-fold (nobilis). The Terrae genus is the journal's formal name for what grey-folk call 'the stone that walks like a person' — the classic bipedal earth elemental recognised across all four known forms.
